largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: Algorithm 1 Source-backed algorithmic procedure
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: Algorithm 1 Source-backed algorithmic procedure

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: 1: Initialize actor network µθ and critic networks Qω1 and Qω2.
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: 1: Initialize actor network µθ and critic networks Qω1 and Qω2.

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: 2: Initialize corresponding target networks: θ′ ←θ, ω′1 ←ω1, ω′2 ←ω2 and choose ρp ∈(0, 1).
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: 2: Initialize corresponding target networks: θ′ ←θ, ω′1 ←ω1, ω′2 ←ω2 and choose ρp ∈(0, 1).

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: 3: Initialize replay buffer R.
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: 3: Initialize replay buffer R.

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: 4: Choose a batch size K, a gradient based optimization algorithm and a corresponding learning rate λactor, λcritic > 0 for both optimization procedures, a time step size ∆t and a
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: 4: Choose a batch size K, a gradient based optimization algorithm and a corresponding learning rate λactor, λcritic > 0 for both optimization procedures, a time step size ∆t and a

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: 5: Choose standard deviation exploration noise σexpl and lower and upper action bounds alow, ahigh.
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: 5: Choose standard deviation exploration noise σexpl and lower and upper action bounds alow, ahigh.

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: 6: repeat
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: 6: repeat

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: 7: Select clipped action and step the environment dynamics forward.
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: 7: Select clipped action and step the environment dynamics forward.

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: a = clip(µθ(s) + ϵ, alow, ahigh), ϵ ∼N(0, σexpl).
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: a = clip(µθ(s) + ϵ, alow, ahigh), ϵ ∼N(0, σexpl).

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: 8: Observe next state s′, reward r, and done signal d and store the tuple (s, a, r, s′, d) in the replay buffer.
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: 8: Observe next state s′, reward r, and done signal d and store the tuple (s, a, r, s′, d) in the replay buffer.

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: 9: if s′ is terminal then
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: 9: if s′ is terminal then

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: 10: Reset trajectory.
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: 10: Reset trajectory.

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: 11: end if
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: 11: end if

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: 12: for j in range(update frequency) do
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: 12: for j in range(update frequency) do

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: 13: Sample batch B = {(s(k), a(k), r(k), s′(k), d(k))}K k=1 from replay buffer.
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: 13: Sample batch B = {(s(k), a(k), r(k), s′(k), d(k))}K k=1 from replay buffer.

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: 14: Compute targets (Clipped Double Q-learning and policy smoothing).
largest_cost_contributions.reference_text: 14: Compute targets (Clipped Double Q-learning and policy smoothing).

largest_cost_contributions.candidate_text: y(r, s′, d) = r + (1 −d) min i=1,2 Qω′i(s′, ˜a) , ˜a = clip(µθ′(s) + ϵ, alow, ahigh), ϵ ∼N(0, σtarget).

evidence.mass_policy: block normalized-character count; minimum mass unit 1
evidence.page_geometry: page boxes stacked vertically and normalized by max page count

block_transport.interpretation: Exact optimal transport over extracted text blocks. This is an LTSim-inspired, fully specified adaptation—not the published LTSim implementation or a semantic table/formula metric. Report geometry and content costs separately with the flows.

canvas.interpretation: Canvas/page-size mismatch only; no token layout residual is folded into this axis.

content.interpretation: Strict NFC preservation is the primary text view. NFKC is reported separately as a compatibility view for ligature/encoding equivalence; it never hides the strict mismatch.

evidence.normalization: Unicode NFKC; case preserved; multiset comparison

critical_content.interpretation: Exact critical-symbol inventories prevent visually small numeric or operator errors from disappearing inside a document-level text average. Empty inventories are reported as exact but do not establish construct applicability.

figures.reason: Applicability for figures was not established by source metadata.

formulas.reason: Applicability for formulas was not established by source metadata.

geometry.interpretation: Token boxes are normalized by each page's own canvas; canvas mismatch remains a separate axis.

worst_block_pairs.reference_text: Algorithmic Pseudocode Sample 13
Source-backed Image2Struct algorithm sample
1 Algorithm
This sample contains algorithmic pseudocode extracted from a source-backed LaTeX benchmark dataset. It is wrapped in a minimal article document for pdfLaTeX val
Algorithm 1 Source-backed algorithmic procedure
1: Initialize actor network µθ and critic networks Qω1 and Qω2.
2: Initialize corresponding target networks: θ′ ←θ, ω′1 ←ω1, ω′2 ←ω2 and choose ρp ∈(0, 1).
3: Initialize replay buffer R.
4: Choose a batch size K, a gradient based optimization algorithm and a corresponding learning rate λactor, λcritic > 0 for both optimization procedures, a time
5: Choose standard deviation exploration noise σexpl and lower and upper action bounds alow, ahigh.
6: repeat
7: Select clipped action and step the environment dynamics forward.
a = clip(µθ(s) + ϵ, alow, ahigh), ϵ ∼N(0, σexpl).
8: Observe next state s′, reward r, and done signal d and store the tuple (s, a, r, s′, d) in the replay buffer.
9: if s′ is terminal then
10: Reset trajectory.
11: end if
12: for j in range(update frequency) do
13: Sample batch B = {(s(k), a(k), r(k), s′(k), d(k))}K k=1 from replay buffer.
14: Compute targets (Clipped Double Q-learning and policy smoothing).
y(r, s′, d) = r + (1 −d) min i=1,2 Qω′i(s′, ˜a) , ˜a = clip(µθ′(s) + ϵ, alow, ahigh), ϵ ∼N(0, σtarget).
15: Estimate critic gradient ∇ωL(Qµθ ω ) by
K X

layout_relations.interpretation: Transparent matched-block relation trial inspired by pairwise-layout work; this is not the published PaIRS or LTSim metric.

pagination.interpretation: Page count, matched-block page assignment, and page-break boundaries are reported independently.

raster_ink.interpretation: Candidate pages are resized to the reference canvas, then optionally translation-registered. Use canvas and geometry axes for layout claims.

raster_perceptual.interpretation: SSIM is reported before and after translation-only registration. The multiscale value is the documented geometric mean of four SSIM scales, not canonical MS-SSIM. No candidate resizing is performed; SSIM abstains when physical canvases/grids differ.

reading_order.interpretation: Kendall tau is computed only on content-matched blocks; coverage must accompany it.

tables.reason: Applicability for tables was not established by source metadata.

evidence.match_policy: exact Unicode-NFKC word text with deterministic page/position assignment for repeats

text_grounding.interpretation: Digital-PDF exact-word grounding adaptation; it is not the published CLEval algorithm. Split/merge values remain null until a validated implementation exists.

evidence.equations: uniform mass; delta_bbox=(1+GIoU)/2; delta_label=1 for text; mu=1-(delta_bbox+delta_label)/2; Text-LTSim=exp(-EMD), sigma=1

text_ltsim.interpretation: Faithful published LTSim equations on extracted text blocks only. It measures text-layout geometry/segmentation, not full semantic layout; non-text classes abstain.

typography.interpretation: Raw PDF font observables are diagnostic; font names and flags are renderer metadata, not perceptual truth.

configuration.normalization: Unicode NFKC, soft-hyphen removal, collapsed whitespace; case preserved

evaluator.name: pdf_metric_axes_v2
evaluator.policy: No compensatory scalar. Report raw axes, coverage, abstentions, and localized evidence.
